DISCOVERY 302, MOTION FOR PROTECTION IS CONTINUED FROM OCT-03-2016 TO OCT-17-2016, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPARTMENT 302, LAW & MOTION. PLAINTIFF IS INFORMED THAT A $30. COURT REPORTER FEE WILL BE ASSESSED AND MUST BE PAID BEFORE THE HEARING DATE. JUDGE PRO TEM TO PREPARE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION. JUDGE PRO TEM: J. TIMOTHY NARDELL; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: JOANNA BROADWELL, CSR NO. 10959, TEL. 415.274.9977; DEPT. 302.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY FAIRFIELD'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER, REGARDING THE DEPOSITION OF HIS TREATING PHYSICIANS IS DENIED IN LARGE PART. DEFENDANT MOZILLA CORPORATION MAY DEPOSE THE TREATING PHYSICIANS AT ANY TIME FROM NOW UNTIL THE CLOSE OF DISCOVERY. THERE IS NO REASON WHY THE TREATING PHYSICIANS, WHO HAVE PERCIPIENT INFORMATION, MAY NOT NOW BE DEPOSED. WHILE IT IS POSSIBLE THAT SOME OF THESE TREATING PHYSICIANS WILL ALSO BE DESIGNATED AS EXPERTS, THAT IN AND OF ITSELF IS NOT A BASIS TO DELAY MOZILLA'S ABILITY TO OBTAIN POTENTIALLY HIGHLY RELEVANT INFORMATION AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE. THOSE DEPOSITIONS MAY PROCEED WHETHER OR NOT MR. FAIRFIELD SIGNS MEDICAL AUTHORIZATIONS. HOWEVER, TO THE EXTENT THAT THE DEPONENTS REVEAL PRIVATE MEDICAL INFORMATION ABOUT MR. FAIRFIELD MOZILLA MAY USE THAT INFORMATION ONLY FOR THIS CASE AND MAY ONLY DISCLOSE IT TO PERSONS WHO HAVE A LEGITIMATE NEED TO KNOW SUCH INFORMATION FOR THE DEFENSE OF THIS CASE. DEFENDANT, MOZILLA CORPORATION'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF, TIMOTHY FAIRFIELD AND HIS COUNSEL, PAYABLE TO DEFENDANT WITHIN 15 DAYS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendants Special Interrogatories, Set Two
Off Calendar Oct-13-2016 Continued To Nov-07-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protection
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Tanya Gomerman and Arcolina Panto of the Law Offices of Tanya Gomerman (415.545.8608) appear as counsels for plaintiff/moving party

Attorneys, Jared L. Palmer and Kevin Reese of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart PC (415.442.4810) appear as counsels for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY FAIRFIELD'S Motion For Protective Order, regarding the deposition of his treating physicians is DENIED in large part. Defendant Mozilla Corporation may depose the treating physicians at any time from now until the close of discovery. There is no reason why the treating physicians, who have percipient information, may not now be deposed. While it is possible that some of these treating physicians will also be designated as experts, that in and of itself is not a basis to delay Mozilla's ability to obtain potentially highly relevant information at this stage of the case. Those depositions may proceed whether or not Mr. Fairfield signs medical authorizations. However, to the extent that the deponents reveal private medical information about Mr. Fairfield Mozilla may use that information only for this case and may only disclose it to persons who have a legitimate need to know such information for the defense of this case. Defendant, Mozilla Corporation's request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED. Sanctions are imposed against plaintiff, Timothy Fairfield and his counsel, payable to defendant within 15 days. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protection
Off Calendar Oct-03-2016 Continued To Oct-17-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Arcolina Panto, of Law Offices of Tanya Gomerman, Tel. 415.545.8608, for plaintiffs;
Kevin Reese, of Ogletree, Deakins, Nash, Smoak & Stewart, P.C., Tel. 415.442.4810, for defendants;
Jared Palner, of Ogletree, Deakins, Nash, Smoak & Stewart, P.C., Tel. 415.442.4810, for defendants
Court Reporter: Joanna Broadwell, CSR No. 10959, Tel. 415.274.9977.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Plaintiff's counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem. PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY FAIRFIELD Motion For Protection is continued to October 17, 2016, at 9:30 a.m., in Department 302, Law & Motion. Plaintiff is informed that a $30. Court Reporter fee will be assessed and must be paid before the hearing date. Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: J. Timothy Nardell; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Joanna Broadwell, CSR No. 10959, Tel. 415.274.9977; Dept. 302.
